takeaways: Market declined (Nifty -2.06%). Look for opportunities in quality stocks with strong fundamentals — dips are for accumulating, not panicking.
Sector rotation: Pharma leads (+2.9% month), Auto lags (-14.3%). Consider tilting new allocation toward sectors with strong momentum.
30 watchlist stocks show accumulate signals: BRITANNIA, ABB, SAFARI, TITAGARH, SBIN. Build positions in 2-3 tranches; max 6% per name.
2 watchlist stocks on wait: GODFRYPHLP, DIXON. Avoid catching falling knives — wait for trend reversal confirmation.
Macro watch: Crude at $98.71, USD/INR at 92.45. Rising crude or weakening INR can pressure IT/pharma margins short-term but often creates accumulation opportunities in exporters.
Position sizing: Max 6% in any single name. Multi-year horizon — don't chase momentum; buy quality on weakness. Tax-efficient if held > 1 year.
learning_questions: Would you like more ETF coverage vs individual stocks?
Any upcoming capital needs that affect your investment horizon?
Do you track FII/DII flows? Should we highlight this more?
